Hotfix lisibilité des tableaux de segmentation (libellés binaires explicites) (#6)

* Add sponsor-facing Streamlit app, survey analysis pipeline and delivery artifacts

### Motivation
- Finalise a sponsor-ready analysis of the internal AI usage survey and provide reproducible artefacts for decision-making and demo.
- Provide a lightweight Streamlit cockpit for executives with a descriptive/prescriptive toggle and a protected prescriptive mode.
- Fix deployment dependency issues so the Streamlit app boots reliably in the target environment.

### Description
- Adds the data analysis pipeline `analyze_survey.py` that loads the exported Excel, normalises columns, computes descriptive stats, runs chi2 tests, generates charts and writes multiple markdown deliverables and `outputs/cleaned_data.csv`.
- Adds a sponsor-facing Streamlit application `app/streamlit_app.py` (plus `app/README.md`) with a global `Description seule` toggle (password-protected prescriptive mode), respondent explorer, segmentations and verbatim thematic views.
- Adds extensive delivery artefacts under `outputs/` (executive summaries, reports, governance/enablement templates, charts, variable dictionary, release notes, demo script, validation and publish status files).
- Fixes Streamlit deployment dependency handling by adding a root `requirements.txt` (includes `streamlit`, `pandas`, `numpy`, `scipy`) and a UI theme file `.streamlit/config.toml`; sample survey Excel is included under the repo for reproducibility.

### Testing
- Ran `python analyze_survey.py` which completed and printed a JSON `status: ok` (rows=70, raw_cols=91, clean_cols=33); this check succeeded.
- Compiled the app with `python -m py_compile app/streamlit_app.py`; compilation succeeded.
- Verified Streamlit app startup with `timeout 20s streamlit run app/streamlit_app.py --server.headless true --server.port 8501`; headless boot was observed and then stopped by the controlled timeout (expected), indicating the app starts without import errors.
- Dependency install check (`pip install -r requirements.txt`) and local import checks passed and resolved previous `scipy` import failures; these validations succeeded.

* Integrate selected COMEX deck narrative into Streamlit app

* Update delivery status for deck integration PR #5

* Fix latest commit in delivery status for PR #5

* Improve segmentation table readability with explicit French binary labels

* Update delivery status for segmentation readability PR #6

* Fix latest commit reference in delivery status for PR #6

fix(sentry): Tighten OAuth setup and dev plugin loading (#179)

Make the example app load the same plugin package set in local dev that
production builds use, and tighten OAuth scope handling for stored user
grants.

The example app was splitting plugin package configuration between Nitro
and `createApp`, which meant local dev could start with a different
plugin set than the production runtime. Separately, stored OAuth tokens
were keyed only by user and provider, so pre-existing Sentry connections
could keep stale scopes and fail later with missing-scope errors after a
seemingly successful reconnect.

This shares the example app plugin package list between Nitro and
`createApp`, keeps the Sentry plugin read-only, persists normalized
granted OAuth scopes with stored tokens, and validates callback,
refresh, and reuse paths against each plugin's required scope.
Under-scoped grants now fail fast and go back through reauthorization
instead of rendering a successful connect flow and failing on the next
tool call.

I considered keeping the reconnect logic only in the Sentry skill, but
that would leave the actual grant validation in the wrong layer and
would still accept obviously insufficient callback or refresh responses.
Validating granted scopes in the OAuth callback and broker keeps the
behavior generic across OAuth-bearer providers while preserving the
Sentry plugin's documented read-only contract.

Split out of the broader `wip/slack-cards-oauth-spike` branch on
purpose. This PR keeps the OAuth and dev-loading fixes separate from the
Slack card experiment.

commit.message:
Hotfix sidebar description toggle and factual/prescriptive navigation (#3)

* Add survey analysis pipeline, sponsor-ready outputs and Streamlit cockpit

### Motivation
- Provide a sponsor-facing package to finalize the IA survey: clean data, produce charts, generate executive deliverables and an interactive Streamlit cockpit for decision-makers.
- Ensure deployability on Streamlit Cloud by declaring runtime dependencies and a UI theme for consistent presentation.
- Capture traceable artifacts (analysis report, one-pager, roadmap, governance, enablement plan, charts and cleaned CSV) for audit and review.

### Description
- Added a data analysis pipeline `analyze_survey.py` that loads the provided Excel survey, normalizes column names, produces `outputs/cleaned_data.csv`, generates charts under `outputs/charts/`, builds `outputs/variable_dictionary.csv` and writes multiple deliverables (`analysis_report.md`, `executive_summary.md`, `one_pager_comex.md`, `board_deck_outline.md`, etc.).
- Added a sponsor-facing Streamlit app in `app/streamlit_app.py` with `app/README.md`, using the generated `outputs` artifacts as sources and a custom theme in `.streamlit/config.toml` for consistent styling.
- Added runtime dependency management via `requirements.txt` including `streamlit`, `pandas`, `numpy`, `scipy` to ensure the app boots in Streamlit Cloud and local environments.
- Included the raw survey file and numerous `outputs/*.md` artifacts (diagnostics, delivery status, reviewer guide, roadmap, enablement plan, governance framework, demo script, etc.) to make the analysis and publication state explicit and reviewable.

### Testing
- Executed the analysis pipeline via `python analyze_survey.py`, which completed and printed a JSON status indicating `"status": "ok"` and `rows=70`, `raw_cols=91`, `clean_cols=33` (success).
- Compiled the Streamlit app with `python -m py_compile app/streamlit_app.py` with no syntax errors (OK).
- Launched Streamlit headless to validate boot with `timeout 20s streamlit run app/streamlit_app.py --server.headless true --server.port 8501`; startup was observed and stopped by the timeout, with no `ModuleNotFoundError` for `scipy` after adding `requirements.txt` (OK).
- Verified existence and sizes of key output artifacts and charts (`outputs/cleaned_data.csv`, `outputs/executive_summary.md`, `outputs/analysis_report.md`, `outputs/variable_dictionary.csv`, `outputs/charts/*.png`) as part of the final quality checks (OK).

* fix(sql): add FOR UPDATE row lock to prevent TOCTOU in checkout reuse

The prepare_storefront_order_for_checkout function reads order state
then updates it non-atomically. A concurrent webhook marking the order
as 'paid' between the SELECT and UPDATE could be silently overwritten
back to 'unpaid'. Adding FOR UPDATE locks the row for the duration of
the function, serializing the check-then-update sequence.

* fix(import): delegate to worker instead of inline processing in after()

Replace kickoffImportJob with startImportJob in all 4 import route
after() callbacks (finalize, multipart upload, commit, notify-customers).

startImportJob uses a clean strategy:
- Production with worker secret: delegates to /api/import-jobs/worker
  (runs in its own request context with maxDuration=300)
- Production without secret: throws to catch misconfiguration
- Non-production (localhost): falls back to inline processing

Previously, after() always ran processImportJobById inline first, which
claims the job then does heavy I/O. On Vercel, the function timeout
kills the process mid-work, orphaning the job with no recovery path.

Also adds logger.warn in triggerImportWorker when IMPORT_JOB_WORKER_SECRET
is missing as a defensive layer.

feat: optimize token usage via category-aware prompts and native tool calling (#40)

* feat: optimize token usage via category-aware prompts and native Anthropic tool calling

- Add ShortDescription to all tool descriptors; format converters accept compact=true
  to use trimmed descriptions in native schemas
- FileSystemSystemPrompt now has builder methods (BuildNativeCompact, BuildTextBased)
  that filter content to only enabled tool categories
- BaseAiProvider.BuildToolSystemPrompt() delegates prompt construction per provider,
  using compact or full variant based on PreferCompactToolDescriptions
- ChatTurnWorkflowService delegates system prompt building to provider instead of
  selecting inline
- OpenAI and Gemini providers opt into compact mode (PreferCompactToolDescriptions=true)
- ClaudeAiProvider switches to native function calling: injects tools array into
  request body, parses tool_use SSE blocks (input_json_delta accumulation)
- Tests: ShortDescription fallback, compact format converters, native tool call
  parsing for Anthropic, BuildToolSystemPrompt delegation

Estimated savings: ~1100-1850 tokens/turn depending on provider and enabled categories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Codex review comments on PR #40

- ClaudeAiProvider: preserve exact JSON types (bool, number, array, object)
  when converting Anthropic tool_use blocks to <tool_call> format. Previously
  non-string values were stored as GetRawText() and re-serialized as strings,
  breaking typed parameters (e.g. append:true became "true").
- FileSystemSystemPrompt: split keyboard/mouse tool listing so only tools from
  the actually-enabled category are advertised in the text-mode prompt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: gate scripting/large-file rules and switch_model on enabled categories

- BuildNativeCompact: SCRIPTING and LARGE FILES rules now only included when
  filesystem category is enabled (they reference write_file/execute_command/read_file)
- BuildTextBased: switch_model tool listing now gated on hasAgent, matching the
  tool schema filtering so the prompt never advertises unavailable tools

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: gate examples and additional rules on enabled categories

- Notepad example (uses execute_command + begin_keyboard_session/type_text) now
  only emitted when both filesystem AND keyboard categories are enabled
- ADDITIONAL RULES split by category: fs-specific rules (paths, read-before-edit,
  where/winget) gated on hasFs; browser rules (open_url, open_browser_tab) gated
  on hasBrowser — prompt never advertises tools not in the active schema

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate 'Switched to' messages on provider list refresh

RefreshProvidersAsync sets _suppressProviderChange=true before calling
LoadProvidersAsync, but LoadProvidersAsync resets the flag to false before
returning. When RefreshProvidersAsync then sets ProviderComboBox.SelectedItem,
the SelectionChanged event fires unsuppressed and calls UpdateCurrentProvider
with showSwitchedMessage:true, showing a spurious 'Switched to X' message even
though the provider hasn't changed.

Fix: re-assert _suppressProviderChange=true immediately after LoadProvidersAsync
returns so the subsequent SelectedItem assignment is silent. The single explicit
UpdateCurrentProviderAsync(showSwitchedMessage:false) call at the end handles
any real provider state update.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: gate fs/browser critical rules and compact keyboard guidance on enabled categories

- BuildNativeCompact: replace hasInput (keyboard||mouse) with separate
  hasKeyboard/hasMouse; gate KEYBOARD-FIRST rule on hasKeyboard only
  (begin_keyboard_session is keyboard-specific, not mouse)
- BuildTextBased CRITICAL RULES: break monolithic string into conditional
  sb.Append() blocks so execute_command/fs rules (2,3,4,9) are gated on
  hasFs and open_url/browser rules (6,7,8) are gated on hasBrowser;
  rules 1/1a/5/5a (always applicable) are unconditional

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: gate scripting/large-file rules and workflow steps on enabled categories

- BuildTextBased SCRIPTING RULE + LARGE FILE RULE: wrapped in hasFs guard
  so browser-only or agent-only configs no longer see write_file/execute_command
  scripting instructions
- BuildTextBased WORKFLOW block: replaced numbered steps with conditional
  bullet appends — execute_command step gated on hasFs&&hasKeyboard,
  take_screenshot step gated on hasMouse, key-names line gated on hasKeyboard

commit.message:
Simplify sidebar navigation to five primary destinations (#174)

* Make navigation fit the five-tab mobile shell

Centralize the recruiter navigation model so the desktop sidebar,
mobile bottom nav, mobile overflow affordance, and command palette
all derive from the same five primary destinations and six Meer
routes. This removes the old desktop/sidebar sprawl while keeping
legacy destinations reachable without breaking direct routes.

The mobile shell now keeps the bottom nav visible on /chat so the
active state stays aligned with the current route, and the desktop
shell surfaces a single Meer trigger instead of exposing secondary
surfaces as top-level sidebar noise.

Constraint: Existing routes had to remain reachable while navigation moved
Constraint: Runtime validation was limited to routes that can render with a temporary local DATABASE_URL placeholder
Rejected: Keep Chat hidden from the mobile bottom nav | violates the active-state requirement on /chat
Rejected: Leave secondary items as visible sidebar groups | keeps the mobile-first information architecture noisy
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: moderate
Directive: Treat components/navigation-config.ts as the single source of truth for app navigation before editing sidebar, mobile nav, or command palette surfaces
Tested: pnpm exec vitest run tests/recruiter-dashboard-navigation.test.ts
Tested: pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
Tested: pnpm lint
Tested: Playwright screenshots on /automatisering and /chat with a temporary DATABASE_URL placeholder
Not-tested: Data-backed primary routes against a real Neon database in this workspace
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Keep CI aligned with the new navigation contract

The navigation simplification moved secondary-route expectations into a
shared config, but one structural shell test was still asserting on the
old inline sidebar literals. Update that regression to follow the new
source of truth so CI evaluates the intended contract instead of the
removed implementation detail.

Constraint: The fix had to preserve the new shared-navigation design rather than reintroduce sidebar duplication just to satisfy CI
Rejected: Put Interviews back into app-sidebar.tsx as an inline literal | would undermine the single-source navigation model
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Directive: When navigation changes move config into components/navigation-config.ts, update structural tests to assert on that module instead of inline sidebar strings
Tested: pnpm test
Not-tested: Remote GitHub checks after push
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Prove the shared navigation matcher behaves as intended

Code review called out that the new helper functions were only covered
indirectly through structural tests. Add direct assertions for exact
matches, nested matches, alias-path matches, and non-matches so the
shared navigation contract is explicit and resistant to future drift.

Constraint: The added coverage had to validate the shared helper behavior without reintroducing per-component test duplication
Rejected: Rely on structural source-string checks alone | does not prove the helper logic used by sidebar and mobile nav
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Directive: When navigation matching logic changes, extend these helper assertions before depending on structural snapshots alone
Tested: pnpm test
Not-tested: Remote GitHub checks after push
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Keep the navigation test follow-up Biome-clean

The helper-coverage follow-up introduced non-null assertions that the
repo lint rules forbid. Replace those assertions with an explicit guard
so the navigation tests stay readable, lint-clean, and aligned with the
CI gate that runs on every PR update.

Constraint: The fix needed to preserve the new helper coverage without weakening the test assertions
Rejected: Disable the lint rule or rely on a stale CI run | would leave the branch failing the actual Symphony gate
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Directive: Prefer explicit guards over non-null assertions in Vitest coverage additions because Biome enforces noNonNullAssertion in this repo
Tested: pnpm lint
Tested: pnpm exec vitest run tests/recruiter-dashboard-navigation.test.ts tests/sidebar-shell-top-bar.test.ts
Not-tested: Full remote CI rerun after push

commit.message:
Make two-pass context phases explicit

Replace the sentinel-based SourceContext phase contract with an
explicit SourceContextRequest object. Two-pass contexts now receive
phase: "phase1" or phase: "phase2" requests, which removes the
ambiguity between the first collection pass and a real successful
parser value of undefined.

Update the core runner plumbing, remove the config-specific undefined
wrapping workaround, and add regression coverage for the undefined
first-pass case. Refresh manual getAnnotations() callers, examples,
and documentation so they use the new phase-two request shape.

Update the changelog and related docs for the breaking API change.

Fixes https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/271
